title: melissa serpyllifolia
intro: Melissa serpyllifolia, also known as the wild bergamot or bee balm, is a perennial plant belonging to the mint family, Lamiaceae. It is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and has been used for centuries in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Melissa serpyllifolia include relief from stress and anxiety, improved sleep quality, and a boost to the immune system. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
